name: best-minds
description: Simulator-mindset methodology skill: don't ask 'what do you think,' ask 'which group of people would best explore this, and what would they say?' Based on Karpathy's 2025 simulator view; includes anti-sycophancy guardrails, quote verification, and a blind-spot scan. Replies follow the user's language.

interface.shortDescription: Round table of the best minds for open-ended questions
interface.longDescription: For open-ended judgment, design trade-offs, and strategy where there is no single right answer: instead of asking the AI what it thinks, convene the group of real people who would best explore the question — map the opposing axes, seat a designated dissenter, simulate each voice's actual public stance with quote verification, and converge with a blind-spot scan.
defaultPrompt: $best-minds should we enforce TDD and 100% coverage across the team?
$best-minds how should an AI customer-service memory system be designed?
$best-minds is a rewrite or an incremental refactor right for this legacy codebase?
